target.name: Aurora Innovation
target.founder: Chris Urmson, Sterling Anderson, Drew Bagnell

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
Aurora Innovation operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector at the public stage, having raised $2B+ to date. The comparable universe in this domain is sparse: six private peers exist (Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Nuro, Elroy Air, ideaForge), but no public comparables are available. This structural constraint significantly limits valuation methodology.

Peer-median analysis is inapplicable here. With only two same-stage peers identified and zero adjacent-stage or similar-tier private companies meeting screening criteria, the dataset falls below the minimum threshold of three datapoints required for a defensible median calculation. Broadening to all four same-sector peers would introduce excessive heterogeneity across business models and maturity levels, rendering the resulting median unreliable for valuation support.

The absence of public market data and the fragmentation of the private peer set leave traditional triangulation methods unsupported by the available data. Any valuation range derived from this analysis would rest on either (1) transaction multiples from the limited private peer set, with acknowledged selection and comparability bias, or (2) DCF methods, which depend on assumptions not provided here. The key uncertainties are the relevance of the five identified private peers to Aurora's specific technology, unit economics, and path to profitability—gaps that cannot be resolved through comps analysis alone. Engagement with management and deeper fundamental due diligence is necessary to anchor valuation.
